findings.title: FolderMonitor copies watchPaths by reference, not by value
findings.description: Encapsulation: 5/10, Expression: 7/10, Usefulness: 7/10, Enforcement: 7/10 (Aggregate: 6.5). The constructor stores a direct reference to the caller's watchPaths array. If the caller mutates the array after construction (e.g., push/pop), the FolderMonitor's internal state changes silently. Combined with FolderMonitorOptions.watchPaths lacking a readonly modifier, this is a classic shared-mutable-state encapsulation leak.
findings.suggestion: Copy the array in the constructor: `this.watchPaths = [...options.watchPaths]`. Also add `readonly` to the FolderMonitorOptions.watchPaths type: `readonly watchPaths: readonly string[]`.

findings.title: Unsafe `as Record<string, unknown>` cast on pdfjs-dist text items
findings.description: Encapsulation: 6/10, Expression: 5/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 5/10 (Aggregate: 5.5). The pdfjs-dist TextContent.items array contains a union type (TextItem | TextMarkedContent). The code uses `'str' in item` as a discriminator but then double-casts through Record<string, unknown> instead of narrowing to TextItem. The `in` check already narrows the type in TypeScript; the `as` casts are redundant and bypass type safety.
findings.suggestion: Use proper type narrowing: `import type { TextItem } from 'pdfjs-dist'`, then check `if ('str' in item) { return (item as TextItem).str }` -- or better, define a type guard `function isTextItem(item: unknown): item is TextItem`.

findings.title: Unsafe `as` cast on exceljs row.values without type guard
findings.code_snippet: const cellValues = row.values as (string | number | boolean | null | undefined)[]
findings.description: Encapsulation: 6/10, Expression: 5/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 4/10 (Aggregate: 5.25). exceljs `row.values` returns `CellValue[]` which includes Date, CellErrorValue, CellRichTextValue, CellHyperlinkValue, CellFormulaValue, and CellSharedFormulaValue -- not just primitives. Casting to `(string | number | boolean | null | undefined)[]` silently discards these complex types. A Date cell would pass String(v) but produce an undesirable format; a formula cell could leak internal structure.
findings.suggestion: Define a helper that handles the full CellValue union: extract the .result for formulas, format Dates explicitly, and convert rich text to plain string. At minimum, use `String(v)` on the original CellValue type without the narrowing cast.

findings.title: FolderMonitorOptions fields lack readonly, inconsistent with output types
findings.description: Encapsulation: 4/10, Expression: 6/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 7/10 (Aggregate: 5.75). The new output types (DocumentExtractionResult, TextChunk, FileChangeEvent) correctly use readonly on all fields -- a significant improvement over earlier codebase types. However, FolderMonitorOptions has mutable watchPaths (string[]) and extensions (string[]) arrays. Since these are configuration inputs consumed by the constructor, they should be `readonly string[]` to prevent accidental mutation and to signal intent. This inconsistency within the same diff is notable.
findings.suggestion: Change to: `readonly watchPaths: readonly string[]` and `readonly extensions?: readonly string[]`. The callback fields (onChange, onError) are fine as-is since functions are not mutated.

findings.title: New output types use readonly consistently -- improved over prior types
findings.description: Encapsulation: 8/10, Expression: 8/10, Usefulness: 7/10, Enforcement: 7/10 (Aggregate: 7.5). DocumentExtractionResult, TextChunk, and FileChangeEvent all use readonly on every field. DocumentType is a proper 4-value literal union. FileChangeEvent.type is a 3-value literal union. TextChunkerOptions constraints are validated in the chunkText pure function. This is the best readonly discipline seen in this codebase and should be the template for future types.
findings.suggestion: Consider applying this same readonly pattern retroactively to older types in types.ts (ActivityEvent, ActivityContext, etc.) which currently lack readonly modifiers.

positive_observations: DocumentExtractionResult, TextChunk, and FileChangeEvent consistently use readonly on all fields -- best practice in this codebase so far
DocumentType is a proper 4-value literal union preventing invalid document types at compile time
FileChangeEvent.type uses a 3-value literal union ('create' | 'update' | 'delete') -- makes illegal states unrepresentable
chunkText is a pure function with thorough input validation (chunkSize > 0, overlapSize >= 0, overlapSize < chunkSize) -- good Functional Core pattern
FolderMonitor constructor validates watchPaths is non-empty and normalizes extensions with dot prefix and lowercase -- good invariant establishment
FolderMonitor is correctly labeled as Imperative Shell and FolderMonitor.start() is idempotent
DocumentProcessor is correctly labeled as Imperative Shell with I/O operations
